title: Tool Registry, Allowlist, Validation, Dispatch, Results, and Unknown Calls · Python Build Room
summary: A public-interest product team incrementally builds allowlisted tool registry and result envelope from original fixed provider, interaction, tool, approval, evaluation, and failure fixtures while retaining prior Python, HTTP, prompt, loop, security, accessibility, testing, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of allowlisted tool registry and result envelope while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory…
objectives: Register immutable tool name, schema version, handler, effect class, policy, timeout, owner, and result contract in one auditable catalog.
Preserve provider call ID, local attempt ID, interaction ID, tool version, arguments digest, result digest, and timestamps.
Validate and normalize model arguments before policy and execution while retaining rejected input evidence safely.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-ai-agent-python cumulative safe evaluated AI agent portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python interaction, tool, policy, state, memory,
evaluation, telemetry, and recovery models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only.
Provider APIs, Gemini models, native google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic, HTTP, credentials,
host files, code execution, browsers, databases, telemetry backends, load, cost, privacy review,
and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentPolicy:
    stakeholder_task_id: str
    allowed_tool_names: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_steps: int = 12
    maximum_tool_calls: int = 8
    approval_required_for_writes: bool = True


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentEvidence:
    revision: str
    model_config_digest: str
    policy_digest: str
    evaluation_digest: str
    recovery_drill_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Safe evaluated Python AI agent portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: agentpy-unknown-tool-rejection-dj7i6y
# Competency: agentpy-unknown-tool-rejection.
# Case 1206: a privacy-minimized nonprofit workflow.
# Operating constraint: telemetry and memory must remain tenant-isolated, minimized, redacted, and attributable.
# Changed case: replace trusted context with untrusted indirect instructions and preserve data-versus-instruction boundaries.
def evidence_agentpy_unknown_tool_rejection_dj7i6y(received_calls: int = 20, dispatched_calls: int = 14, rejected_calls: int = 6, correlated_results: int = 20, duplicate_dispatches: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_dj7i6y = "dj7i6y-y6i7jd-1206"
    assert evidence_variant_dj7i6y
    evidence_axes_dj7i6y = (
        "authoritydj7i6y",
        "interaction1206",
        "tooly6i7jd",
        "statedj7i6y1206",
        "evaluation1206dj7i6y",
        "recoveryy6i7jd1206",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_dj7i6y)) == 6
    if received_calls < 1 or dispatched_calls < 0 or rejected_calls < 0:
        return False, received_calls, "dispatch-count"
    if dispatched_calls + rejected_calls != received_calls:
        return False, dispatched_calls, "dispatch-reconciliation"
    if correlated_results != received_calls:
        return False, correlated_results, "result-correlation"
    if duplicate_dispatches > 0:
        return False, duplicate_dispatches, "duplicate-dispatch"
    assert dispatched_calls + rejected_calls == received_calls and duplicate_dispatches == 0
    return True, dispatched_calls, "allowlisted-tool-dispatch"
